subject: LU-8117 build: fix lustre-ppc64 build on el7

values.-1: I would prefer that you didn't submit this
values.+1: Looks good to me, but someone else must approve

messages.message: Uploaded patch set 1.

Patch Set 1:

Looks good to me.

Patch Set 1:

Not sure this mod does the right thing.  Don't we want the vendor provided flags to be used by default for kernel module builds?   Seems the natural thing to do.

Even if it does fix the ppc build I'm worried that this isn't a proper fix and could have undesirable side effects.

Patch Set 1:

Code-wise looks good to me.

Patch Set 1:

PPC64 Builder: build succesful

Patch Set 1:

They are cleared anyway. Fr example, you can see in kernel arch/arm/Makefile:
# Ensure linker flags are correct
LDFLAGS         :=

parents.subject: LU-7099 lnet: lock improvement for ko2iblnd

commit.subject: LU-8117 build: fix lustre-ppc64 build on el7
commit.message:
LU-8117 build: fix lustre-ppc64 build on el7

Explicitly clear the LDFLAGS for kernel modules build so that
the vendor's overzealous linker flags don't create build failures.

Test-Parameters: trivial

Change-Id: I427cffb229a19a8ff0c2ba0c3ed2ac135f21e24d
Signed-off-by: Dmitry Eremin <dmitry.eremin@intel.com>
Reviewed-on: http://review.whamcloud.com/20404
Tested-by: Jenkins
Reviewed-by: James Simmons <uja.ornl@yahoo.com>
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Minh Diep <minh.diep@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <oleg.drokin@intel.com>
